commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Svelte web consumer for `agent.progress` events, renders ephemeral activity lines in `ChatApp`, and regenerates the embedded web assets.

Reproducibility: yes. By source inspection, current-main producer coverage emits a partial finalize frame, while the PR handler drops frames without `text` or `toolName` before marking the existing line finalized.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blockers are narrow, mechanical web fixes that an automated repair pass can attempt on the PR branch.

target.title: feat(web): render agent.progress as ephemeral activity lines
target.body:
## Consumer half of the `agent.progress` producer/consumer contract

This is the **web client consumer** for the `agent.progress` ephemeral event. The **producer + transport + authorization** primitive landed in #23 (squash-merged to `main` as `feat: add agent progress realtime events`); this PR renders what that primitive delivers. It adds no server behavior.

### Clean web-consumer delta
Now that #23 is merged, this PR is rebased onto current `main` and is a single commit / **13-file web delta** — no server files:
- `apps/web/src/components/messages/AgentProgress.svelte` (new)
- `apps/web/src/ChatApp.svelte`
- the regenerated embedded `apps/api/internal/webassets/dist/*` bundle

### What it does
- **`AgentProgress.svelte`** (new): per-turn line list with kind icons (`tool ⚙`, `thinking/commentary ✦`, `plan ☰`, `patch ±`, `command_output ›`, `error ✕`); finalized lines dim to 0.55; strip caps at ~132px and ellipsizes. Rendered in an accented strip between the message list and composer, mirroring the existing `TypingIndicator` slot.
- **`ChatApp.svelte`** (+68): consumes the `agent.progress` realtime event — `op` append/update/finalize/clear keyed by `turn_id` + `line.id`, scoped to the active channel/DM view only, cleared on view switch, 45s TTL sweeper mirroring the typing-indicator pattern.
- **Ephemeral by construction**: nothing is persisted; reconnect/reload starts clean, matching the event's transport semantics in #23.

### Verification (rebased onto merged `main`)
| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| `cd apps/web && pnpm typecheck` | exit 0 |
| `pnpm build:web && pnpm embed:webassets` | dist regenerated from merged source; byte-identical to committed bundle; `agent-progress` CSS + handler present |
| `go build ./...` (embeds webassets) | exit 0 |
| `go test ./apps/api/internal/httpapi/ -run AgentProgress -count=1` | PASS (5.3s) — against the merged **bot-write** authorization contract |

The render commit only touches the two web source files; the rebase onto merged `main` produced no source conflict, and the regenerated `webassets/dist/*` is reproducible (not hand-merged).

### Visual — real running-app before/after
A controlled before/after capture of the **real clickclack web client** (headless Chromium → dev-bootstrap server → SQLite), driven by **real `agent.progress` frames over the websocket** through the live producer endpoint with a bot token, is posted in the comments. Both panels share the same data dir / workspace / channel / posted turn; the only variable is the embedded web bundle:

- **BEFORE** (stock `main`): frames hit the bus, no consumer — channel stays empty.
- **AFTER** (this PR): frames render as live activity lines, then dim on finalize.

Same input, opposite outcome — that is exactly what this PR adds.
target.author: ragesaq

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
